steps.title: Identify the defining conclusion about cause
steps.content: The key signal is the final statement: “greater proximity alone can't explain the difference in park use.” This tells us directly that something other than proximity must be causing Quito's higher park usage rate compared to Lima's.

steps.title: Match the causal claim to the answer
steps.content:
Choice D fits the passage's reasoning—the difference must be caused by something other than proximity to parks.

The other choices introduce factors never discussed in the text—unexpected results, data inaccuracies, and outdated sources.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
